zh.lifeSummary: 當Maud de Gaunt於1114年出生在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾福爾金哈姆時，她的父親Walter De Gaunt Earl of Lincoln34歲，母親Maud de Penthièvre27歲。她和William de Welles至少有2個兒子和2個女兒。她去世於1176年在她的家鄉享年62歲，埋葬在英國。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Gaunt
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Gant
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Ghent in Flanders, Belgium.
English: from Middle English gaunt, gant ‘slim, slender; drawn, thin and angular’.
North German: probably a variant of   Gans   ‘goose’.

variants.name: Gauntt
meanings: Probably an altered form of   Gaunt  .

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

en.lifeSummary: When Maud de Gaunt was born in 1114, in Folkingham, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Walter De Gaunt Earl of Lincoln, was 34 and her mother, Maud de Penthièvre, was 27. She had at least 2 sons and 2 daughters with William de Welles. She died in 1176, in her hometown, at the age of 62, and was buried in England.

fr.Wells, Norfolk, England: Wells next the Sea, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Maud de Gaunt est née en 1114, à Folkingham, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Walter De Gaunt Earl of Lincoln, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Maud de Penthièvre, avait 27 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles avec William de Welles. Elle est décédée en 1176, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterrée à Angleterre.

de.Wells, Norfolk, England: Wells next the Sea, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als Maud de Gaunt 1114 in Folkingham, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Walter De Gaunt Earl of Lincoln 34 und ihre Mutter Maud de Penthièvre 27. Sie hatte mit William de Welles mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1176 im Alter von 62 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in England begraben.
